content.text:
{
  "dimension": "intervention-quality",
  "score": 3,
  "rationale": "The coach's moves are tightly matched to live reads and sequenced along the AoA path. The opening 'How about right now?' (T4) inverts the future-frame at the exact moment the client posits it. When the client deflects with the second question (T7), the coach holds the first thread and names the said-vs-shown split (T8). When the client calibrates pace ('hold off on the insights'), the coach simply receives with 'Okay' (T14) rather than defending — a precise meet-resistance-with-okay move. When pain surfaces, the coach doesn't coach it; he sits with it ('I'm just sorry,' T22), then returns to work via 'enjoy the sadness' (T24). The shame sequence (T26–T36) demonstrates earned moves: affirms victim-status without shame, surfaces the projection, then applies the signature 'how would you enjoy X / how would you NOT enjoy X' diagnostic pair. The final experiment framing (T40) is offered as something to find out, not prescribed. Timing follows feeling→want→experiment, and the coach pivots angles when needed rather than repeating.",
  "evidence_turns": [4, 8, 14, 16, 22, 24, 26, 30, 34, 36, 40],
  "turn_annotations": [
    {"turn": 4, "polarity": "+", "annotation": "Signature inversion at the exact moment the future-frame is posited — matches the read perfectly."},
    {"turn": 8, "polarity": "+", "annotation": "Holds the first thread, names said-vs-shown discrepancy — precise read-to-move match."},
    {"turn": 12, "polarity": "+", "annotation": "Applies the 'can you enjoy this too' move to oscillation itself."},
    {"turn": 14, "polarity": "+", "annotation": "Meets the client's pacing request with 'Okay' — no defense, no push. High-quality timing move."},
    {"turn": 16, "polarity": "+", "annotation": "Reframes rush itself as potentially enjoyable rather than fixing it."},
    {"turn": 22, "polarity": "+", "annotation": "Drops technique entirely and sits with pain — correct move for the moment."},
    {"turn": 24, "polarity": "+", "annotation": "Returns to work via 'enjoy the sadness,' naming the bracing he notices in the body."},
    {"turn": 26, "polarity": "+", "annotation": "Affirms victim-status the client is resisting — dont-shame-the-grief-process applied."},
    {"turn": 30, "polarity": "+", "annotation": "Uses self-disclosure to flip the projection at the right moment after shame has surfaced."},
    {"turn": 34, "polarity": "+", "annotation": "Signature 'how would you enjoy X' applied directly at shame after it's been earned."},
    {"turn": 36, "polarity": "+", "annotation": "Inverts the prior question to surface the mechanism — diagnostic pair lands."},
    {"turn": 40, "polarity": "+", "annotation": "Frames as an experiment the client can find out, not a prescription."}
  ],
  "expected_territory_hits": ["frame-it-as-an-experiment", "calibrate-the-threshold", "treat-yourself-as-actor-of-emotion", "are-you-bracing-or-embracing", "shame-as-disconnection", "feel-your-way-to-freedom", "natural-shame-vs-installed-shame", "dont-shame-your-grief-process", "loving-to-transform", "fixing-people"],
  "expected_territory_misses": ["how-is-that-not-here-right-now", "what-am-i", "suffering-vs-pain"],
  "notes": "The T14 'Okay' and T22 'I'm sorry' are the strongest evidence of intervention quality — these are moves of restraint that most coaches miss."
}
